audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for first six seals as symbolic exposure of conquest, conflict, scarcity, death, martyr-cry, cosmic disruption.

verses.restored: And I saw when the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures saying like thunder: Come.
And I saw a white horse, and the one sitting on it had a bow; a crown was given to him, and he went out conquering and to conquer.
When he opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature saying: Come.
And another horse, fiery red, went out; to the one sitting on it was given to take peace from the earth so that they would slaughter one another, and a great sword was given.
When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature saying: Come. And I saw a black horse, and the one sitting had scales in his hand.
And I heard as a voice among the four living creatures saying: A quart of wheat for a denarius, and three quarts of barley for a denarius; and do not harm the oil and wine.
When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature saying: Come.
And I saw a pale-green horse, and the one sitting upon it had name Death, and Hades followed; authority was given over a fourth of earth, to kill by sword, famine, death, and beasts of earth.
When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls/lives of those slain because of word of Divine and witness they held.
They cried with great voice: Until when, holy and true Master, do you not judge and avenge our blood/life from those dwelling on earth?
And to each was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until the number of their fellow servants and siblings should be completed.
And I saw when he opened the sixth seal: a great shaking happened, sun became black as sackcloth, and whole moon became like blood.
And stars of heaven fell to earth as fig tree casts unripe figs when shaken by great wind.
Heaven was split like scroll being rolled, and every mountain and island were moved from their places.
Kings of earth and great ones and commanders and rich and strong, and every enslaved and free one, hid themselves in caves and rocks of mountains,
saying to mountains and rocks: Fall on us and hide us from face of the One sitting on throne and from wrath of the Lamb,
because the great day of their wrath came, and who is able to stand?

translation_notes: The seals unveil consequences of imperial conquest: conflict, scarcity, death, martyr-cry, cosmic upheaval.
Wrath of the Lamb is paradoxical apocalyptic justice, not personal vengeance fantasy.
Do not map riders directly onto modern persons or events.
safety_note: Read the horsemen symbolically as exposure of destructive systems; do not use them for fearmongering or target predictions.
